what_it_does: Declares a planned desktop surface specification for view A4 in area A, titled 'Node config — field kinds', binding it to journey J1, a set of UI addresses, and build targets for canvas, inspector, and models components.
observations: It is a generated JSON surface-spec with a self-documenting _what field explaining its schema and provenance.
The surface represents both NODE-config and MOD-registry concepts and is scoped to the desktop platform.
It belongs to a single home journey J1 with no cross_journeys entries.
It lists seven ui:// addresses under ui://canvas/node/inspect, ui://inspector/*, and ui://models.
The build_to block names components canvas, inspector, and models; a design lint command; and a token-to-CSS source path.
Status is 'planned', indicating it is a design substrate rather than a shipped surface.
standouts: The title and represents tags tightly couple the surface to node configuration and module registry concerns.
build_to bundles component scope, a lint gate, and a token source into one object.
All addresses are prefixed with ui:// and grouped around canvas node inspection and the inspector/model areas.
conventions: Follows the generated surface-spec schema: id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, build_to.
Directory path mirrors home_journey (J1) and surface id (A4).
Uses ui:// URI-style addresses and avoids invented paths.
Includes a human-readable _what preamble describing the file's role and constraints.
concerns: cross_journeys is empty despite the preamble describing journeys/cross_journeys as the web that carries B3.
build_to.design_lint is a shell-style command with relative paths and no documented working directory.
build_to.tokens uses an arrow character (→) in a string value, which is readable but not machine-parseable.
notes: This is the planned desktop surface spec for configuring node fields, anchored to journey J1 and feeding canvas/inspector/models build targets.
questions: What is the exact relationship between NODE-config and MOD-registry in this surface?
How are the seven addresses ordered or prioritized?
What do 'the FORM gate' and 'the only look-source' enforce beyond the literal strings?
Which other surfaces reference A4 or share these addresses?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a generated surface-spec for view A4 carrying address-refs; the file does exactly that and adds build-to metadata.
intention_signals: It is trying to define a single, addressable design surface for node configuration so that downstream canvas, inspector, and models components can align on a shared planned spec, lint target, and token source.
novelty: The _what field acts as an inline schema contract and provenance note, and build_to bundles a lint command, component list, and token pipeline in one surface record.
contribution: A concrete planned surface binding for view A4 that links a journey, UI addresses, represented concepts, and build targets, providing a stable substrate for canvas/inspector/models integration.
summary_for_embedding: company/design/blueprint/surfaces/J1/A4.json is a planned desktop surface specification for view A4 ('Node config — field kinds') in area A, tied to journey J1, representing NODE-config and MOD-registry, listing seven ui:// addresses, and targeting canvas, inspector, and models build outputs with a design lint gate and token-to-CSS source.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to record the generated address map and build scope for a node-configuration surface so that canvas, inspector, and model components can share a common planned design substrate.
